rules.rationale: Backend errors crossing public boundaries leak implementation contracts and unstable classification.
rules.remediation: Map the backend failure to a stable package-owned error before returning it.

backend_error_boundaries.description: Exact packages or trailing /... trees that own exported API boundaries.

backend_error_passthroughs.description: Exact wrapper results and argument positions that preserve backend errors.

backend_error_sources.description: Exact backend callables and zero-based error result positions.

rules.rationale: Repository-specific forbidden APIs preserve deprecated dependencies and boundary bypasses.
rules.remediation: Use the configured replacement or move the call into a reviewed adapter.

policies.description: Exact package symbols, replacements, and reviewed package exceptions.

rules.rationale: Organization interface names communicate stable architectural roles at API boundaries.
rules.remediation: Rename the interface to match its configured role or add an exact reviewed compatibility name.

interface_names.description: Package trees with required interface prefixes, suffixes, and exact compatibility names.

rules.rationale: Provider-owned interfaces couple consumers to abstractions they do not require.
rules.remediation: Declare the smallest interface in the consuming package and accept it at the use site.

interface_provider_packages.description: Provider package trees forbidden from declaring exported value interfaces.

rules.rationale: Boundary bypasses couple inner packages to forbidden implementation details.
rules.remediation: Move the dependency behind an approved port or adapter.

contexts.description: Named bounded contexts and the other contexts each may import.

layers.description: Named layers and the other layers each may import.

packages.description: Non-overlapping package trees assigned to layers and contexts.

policies.description: Package trees with explicit denied and allowed imports.

restricted_dependencies.description: Backend package trees and the adapter trees allowed to import them.

rules.rationale: Blocking APIs without context cannot propagate cancellation or deadlines.
rules.remediation: Accept context.Context and propagate it through blocking operations.

policies.description: Exact packages and exported blocking API symbols.

rules.rationale: Replacing an available lifecycle with a root context loses cancellation and deadlines.
rules.remediation: Propagate the caller context or create roots only in approved composition packages.

allowed_packages.description: Exact package paths allowed to create root contexts.

rules.rationale: Struct storage obscures who owns context cancellation and lifetime.
rules.remediation: Pass context through each operation instead of retaining it.

allowed_packages.description: Exact packages with reviewed context ownership.

rules.rationale: HTTP clients without explicit positive timeouts can retain blocked operations and resources indefinitely.
rules.remediation: Set Client.Timeout to a positive reviewed duration, or isolate an intentional streaming client in an approved package.

http_timeout_exceptions.description: Exact packages allowed to construct clients without a positive timeout.

rules.rationale: Process-global HTTP defaults hide connection, timeout, and shutdown ownership.
rules.remediation: Construct and inject an explicit http.Client and cloned transport with reviewed timeouts.

rules.rationale: Discarding an explicit cleanup result loses resource lifetime ownership.
rules.remediation: Bind the cleanup result and call it, defer it, or transfer it to the resource owner.

resource_constructors.description: Exact callables and zero-based cleanup result positions.

rules.rationale: Callbacks and blocking I/O under an internal lock can deadlock, amplify contention, and transfer lock-order control.
rules.remediation: Copy required state, release the lock, then invoke the configured callback or I/O operation.

lock_sensitive_calls.description: Exact callback or I/O callables forbidden while a lock is definitely held.

rules.rationale: Constructors that start goroutines hide lifetime, shutdown, and error ownership from callers.
rules.remediation: Start work explicitly after construction and expose cancellation, waiting, and error contracts.

policies.description: Exact packages and constructor function or method symbols.

rules.rationale: Goroutines started during package initialization have no caller-visible lifetime, shutdown, or error owner.
rules.remediation: Move startup behind explicit construction or Start and expose cancellation, waiting, and error contracts.

rules.rationale: Package init hides ordering, failure, and lifecycle ownership.
rules.remediation: Expose an explicit constructor called by the composition root.

allowed_packages.description: Exact package paths with reviewed init requirements.

rules.rationale: Library process termination bypasses caller-owned error handling and cleanup.
rules.remediation: Return a classified error and let an approved entrypoint terminate the process.

allowed_packages.description: Exact package import paths allowed to control the process.

rules.rationale: A transaction without immediate rollback ownership can remain open on early returns and failed commits.
rules.remediation: After the constructor error guard, immediately defer Rollback on the transaction before performing work.

transactions.description: Exact constructors, transaction result positions, and rollback methods.

rules.rationale: Loop-driven goroutine launches can make concurrency scale with untrusted or unbounded work.
rules.remediation: Use a fixed worker pool, a proven bounded semaphore, or synchronous per-iteration completion.

goroutine_fanout.description: Package trees and maximum statically expanded goroutine launches.

rules.rationale: Attacker-controlled metric label names create unbounded metric schemas and time series.
rules.remediation: Map the input to a fixed allowlist of label names or use it only as a bounded label value.

metric_label_name_sinks.description: Exact metric callables and zero-based label-name positions.

metric_label_name_types.description: Exact named types carrying attacker-controlled label names.

rules.rationale: High-cardinality metric labels create unbounded time series and observability cost.
rules.remediation: Replace the value with a bounded category or remove it from metric labels.

metric_label_sinks.description: Exact metric callables and zero-based label argument positions.

metric_label_types.description: Exact named types carrying high-cardinality label values.

rules.rationale: Composite package variables hide shared ownership and mutable process state.
rules.remediation: Construct the state explicitly and inject its owner into dependent components.

mutable_globals.description: Package trees governed by shared mutable state policy.

rules.rationale: Unsafe, cgo, and linkname bypass Go safety boundaries.
rules.remediation: Use a safe Go API or isolate the operation in an approved package.

rules.rationale: Sensitive typed values can leak through logs, telemetry, errors, formatting, and URLs.
rules.remediation: Pass an explicitly redacted representation or remove the value from the configured sink.

sensitive_types.description: Exact named types carrying secret-bearing values.

sinks.description: Exact callables and sensitive argument positions.

diagnostics.message: lifecycle/no-global-goroutine: package initializer starts a goroutine without caller-visible ownership
diagnostics.rationale: Goroutines started during package initialization have no caller-visible lifetime, shutdown, or error owner.
diagnostics.remediation: Move startup behind explicit construction or Start and expose cancellation, waiting, and error contracts.

diagnostics.message: lifecycle/no-init: package init hides construction and lifecycle ownership
diagnostics.rationale: Package init hides ordering, failure, and lifecycle ownership.
diagnostics.remediation: Expose an explicit constructor called by the composition root.
